name: Poshpacker Hotel
detail: I stayed at women's floor. Among lots of hotel in Taipei, the most important thing to me is the cleaness and easy access to station. This hotel is not only good location but also it was very clean and tidy. The room & bed was compact, but to me, there was no problem. I could stay so pleasantly. The first floor is coffee shop; wake up, go down stair, get a cup of offee easily, it is very nice. Around the hotel, there are food shops, book store and department store. You have got lots options to go&eat.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Caesar Park Hotel Banqiao
detail: Facilities were up to date, rooms were clean and spacious.

name: HOTEL CHAM CHAM - Taipei
detail: Visited in Oct 2019. Hotel has the bare minimum (no pool, no gym) I need for my trip which mostly revolves travelling out to other areas in Taipei. + Toilet has a bidet + Both a shower and tub is present + TV channels were from a TV box and were more than adequate. + Hotel and rooms have a modern design + Attentive staff who replaced the shower and soap when we're almost done with it + Halloween decorations + blood (tomato) juice available at reception Conveniently located near Banqiao station which has 3 main lines - HSR, TRA and Taipei Metro. Makes it easy to get to airport via HSR (+1 transfer onto the Airport Line) and to Taipei Main Station. Located on the Blue Taipei Metro line, Ximen is just a few stops away. Breakfast is not an issue as there is a shop that sells cheap traditional Taiwanese breakfast right opposite the hotel. 2 Family Marts located beside the Hotel and a larger one located in Banqiao station which sells all the souvenir that you need (e.g. ChiaTe)

name: Single Inn Taipei
detail: My first trip to Taiwan and I focused on Taipei area only. Stayed at Single Inn for 7 nights. Even though room may be small, the bed is comfortable with towel and face towel provided. Toilet very clean with shower foam, shampoo, hand washing foam, hair dryer all provided. Breakfast also provided. Extremely value for money for the solo travellers. Less than 5 minutes from FuZhong MRT station even though this station is 6 stations from the Taipei Main Station, not a big issue. There is a convenient store along the way. Highly recommended.

name: TWIN STAR INN
detail: It’s hard to beat this level of accessibility in Taipei.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Work Inn 101
detail: This has just become my go to accommodation for Taipei.

name: WORK INN TPE
detail: Over-all, the hostel is a good option if you are a solo traveler looking for a cheap place to stay in Taiwan. It is walking distance from the Taipei Main Station so going around Taipei is not a problem. My main issue is that the bathroom for the ladies' solo rooms do not have any enclosure. I unfortunately stayed on the private bunk just beside the common bathroom and I was not able to get any sleep because I can hear EVERY noise from the CR (noisy hairdryer and guests who come in as late as 3am). There are signs which say not to talk loudly after 11 pm but some guests just do NOT give a damn about these rules and would chat loudly with their friends while taking a shower. If ever you will book at this hostel, request to get a room as far away from the common bathroom as possible.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Jing Cheng Youth Hostel
detail: The place is walking distance to Zhongshan Station MRT. You can also walk Taipei main station for the airport train express. The hostel offers included breakfast of ham and egg sandwich with seasonal fruits and a hot orccold drink. The reception is also cozy where you can dine or chat with friends, watch tv while surfing the web. As per the toilet and shower, it’s shared but there are enough shower and toilet rooms. You can also wash and dry your clothes with a fee; towel is also with a fee of 30NTD with a 50NTD deposit. It is a good value for money though you there is a need for air freshener in the rooms. If ever you come early or leave late for check-out, you can leave your luggages in the reception area while you can go tour aorund taiwan. Thank you strabox hostel.

name: NYS LOFT HOTEL
detail: We went in group of 4 (3 friends and an elder mother) in a 7-day trip to Taiwan, with first three days staying at NYS LOFT HOTEL in Taipei. I would say that this staying goes beyonds our expectation for a three-star hotel. (1) Nice and helpful staff with fluent English. They even helped us exchange money at late night when we run out of cash and needed to go early on the following morning to Chiayi. They also allowed us to leave our big luggage at the checkin counter during 2 days we were on our trip to Chiayi. (2) Utilities: Free umbrellas to use whenever you need. Free coffee, tea (on self-serving). Spacious common room for kitchen and living room, with oven, glasses, dishes, and bows (wash yourselves after use). (3) Room services: quite spacious (in the description it writes 9 square meters for double room, but I think it is bigger. All facilities are modern and work well (hot water, air conditioner, mini fridge, mini bar, hair-dresser, etc.) and convenient to use; quiet and clean. We booked 2 rooms for 4 people, and both the rooms are excellent. (4) Location: excellent since it takes only 3 minutes walking to the City Main station. The neighbourhood is packed with lots of local shops, foodstores, convenient stores (7-11 stores, family marts, Life OK,etc. which open 24/7). I definitely book this hotel again for my next trips to Taipei as well as recommend it to my friends :)
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: TW Capsule Hostel
detail: If you have a budget for your stay in Taipei, hostel or air bnb might be your option. But often I need a personal space to rest, meaning with no random stranger coming to you to chat up. This capsule accommodation is suitable for solo traveler like me. It is located in Ximending, Taipei (near movie street). But check in have to be at another building.. I was there early around 7.30am and the recept is locked. Is good to take note of the contact and call them. The staff gave me a passcode to leave my baggage inside the lobby first and come back after 8.30 to check in. Staff brought me to another building across one street down. The hostel is at level 3 and is armed with security passcode, and the room of my capsule is armed with another set of passcode. To me it is safe, as it prevents other travelers to enter your room. There are 4 - 6 capsules in a room, stack up two in a block. Mine is on the floor level, which is easier for me to get in and out. The capsule is also secured with a door card to lock and activate the capsule power and AC. The room is big enough for my cabin baggage, my backpack and myself. There are two USB charging points, mirror, small tv with a headphone for volume, two small hooks to hang your clothes or bag. The aircon temperature is moderate. It is comfortable to sleep and relax. But if you have fellow roommates that comes in and out of the capsule pulling the door, it can be very annoying as the capsule is not soundproof. Therefore some very loud snoring can be heard too. The common bathroom is clean except for the water temperature is pretty unpredictable. The price for this capsule is really affordable but I hope there can be a better methods of storing your luggage in a more secure manners. Although men and women room are different, but they still use the common bathroom. It will be better to separate as it might be uncomfortable to see some men watching the female guests coming out of the shower room.
